messages.description1: Alert deduplication is the first layer of denoising. It groups similar alerts from one source.
messages.description2: To connect alerts across sources into incidents, check
messages.activeHint: This page will become active once the first alerts are registered.
messages.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this deduplication rule?

tooltips.distribution: Displays the number of alerts processed hourly over the last 24 hours. A consistent or high distribution indicates steady activity for this deduplication rule.
tooltips.dedupRatio: Represents the percentage of alerts successfully deduplicated. Higher values indicate better deduplication efficiency, meaning fewer redundant alerts.

sidebar.editingDefaultDescription: Editing a default deduplication rule requires advanced knowledge. Default rules are carefully designed to provide optimal deduplication for specific alert types. Modifying these rules may impact the efficiency of your alert processing. If you're unsure about making changes, we recommend creating a new custom rule instead of modifying the default one.
sidebar.editingProvisionedDescription: Editing a provisioned deduplication rule is not allowed. Please contact your system administrator for more information.
sidebar.providerTooltip: Select the provider for which this deduplication rule will apply. This determines the source of alerts that will be processed by this rule.
sidebar.fingerprintFieldsTooltip: Fingerprint fields are used to identify and group similar alerts. Choose fields that uniquely identify an alert type, such as 'service', 'error_type', or 'affected_component'.
sidebar.fingerprintFieldsRequired: At least one fingerprint field is required
sidebar.chooseProviderFirst: Please choose provider to see available fields
sidebar.fullDeduplicationTooltip: 1. Full deduplication: Keep will discard events if they are the same (excluding the 'Ignore Fields'). 2. Partial deduplication (default): Uses specified fields to correlate alerts. E.g., two alerts with same 'service' and 'env' fields will be deduped into one alert.

messages.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this rule?

aiPlugins.title: AI Plugins
aiPlugins.subtitle: For correlation, summarization, and enrichment
aiPlugins.noAIEnabled: No AI enabled for this tenant
aiPlugins.description1: AI plugins can correlate, enrich, or summarize your alerts and incidents by leveraging the the context within Keep allowing you to gain deeper insights and respond more effectively.
aiPlugins.description2: By the way, AI plugins are designed to work even in air-gapped environments. You can train models using your data, so there is no need to share information with third-party providers like OpenAI. Keep your data secure and private.
aiPlugins.contactUs: Talk to us to get access!
aiPlugins.proposalDescription: The last time the model was trained and used for inference, it suggested a configuration update. However, please note that a configuration update might not be very effective if the data quantity or quality is low. For more details, please refer to the logs below.

smtp.title: SMTP Settings
smtp.subtitle: Configure your SMTP server to send emails
smtp.hostDescription: The SMTP host name of your mail server.
smtp.portDescription: SMTP port number to use. Default is 25.
smtp.fromAddressDescription: The default address this server will use to send the emails from.
smtp.usernameDescription: Optional - if you use authenticated SMTP, enter your username.
smtp.passwordDescription: Optional - if you use authenticated SMTP, enter your password.
smtp.toEmailDescription: A test mail address. Keep will try to send email to this address.
smtp.connectionFailed: Failed to connect to server or process the request.

form.appendIncident: Append to the same Incident if delay between alerts is below
form.appendIncidentTooltip: When the first alert arrives, Keep calculates the timespan. Any new alert within this timeframe will correlate into the same incident. The timeframe cannot exceed 90 days.
form.incidentNameTemplateTooltip: You can use alert fields in the template by wrapping them in curly braces, e.g. 'Incident on hosts {{ alert.host }}'. With two alerts from hosts 'host1' and 'host2', the incident name would be 'Incident on hosts host1, host2'. Default: correlation rule name will be used.
form.incidentNameTemplatePlaceholder: Use Keep's expressions to create the incident name, for example: 'Incident on hosts {{ alert.host }}' will create an incident name like 'Incident on hosts host1, host2'. Default: correlation rule name will be used.
form.incidentPrefixTooltip: Incident prefix will be added to the incident name with a running number. For example, if the incident prefix is ACME and the incident name is 'Incident on hosts host1, host2', the incident name will be 'ACME-1 - Incident on hosts host1, host2'.
form.incidentPrefixTooLong: Incident prefix must be less than 10 characters
form.incidentPrefixInvalid: Incident prefix must contain only letters and numbers
form.groupByTooltip: Attributes are used to distinguish between incidents. For example, grouping by 'host' will correlate alerts with hostX and hostY into separate incidents. Attributes cannot be calculated without alerts.
form.multiLevelPropertyNameTooltip: The property name to use for the multi-level correlation. For example, if the property name is 'host', the correlation will be 'host1, host2'.
